info.title: PKI Manager REST API
info.description:
Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) Management System REST API.

This API provides RESTful endpoints for managing Certificate Authorities (CAs),
certificates, Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs), and related operations.

## OpenAPI Specification

The OpenAPI 3.1 specification is available at:
- **JSON**: `/api/v1/openapi.json`
- **YAML**: `/api/v1/openapi.yaml`

Use these URLs to integrate with third-party tools, code generators, or API clients.

## Features
- Certificate Authority management (create, revoke, delete)
- Certificate lifecycle management (issue, renew, revoke, download)
- Bulk operations for certificates
- CRL generation and distribution
- Audit logging and reporting
- Global search across all entities

## Authentication

Most API endpoints require authentication via OAuth2/OIDC Bearer tokens.

### Obtaining a Token

**For users (web application):** Authentication is handled automatically via the web UI using Authorization Code flow with PKCE.

**For machine-to-machine (M2M) access:** Use the Client Credentials flow:

```bash
curl -X POST "http://keycloak:8080/realms/pki-dev/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials" \
  -d "client_id=pki-service" \
  -d "client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"
```

### Using the Token

Include the token in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
```

### Public Endpoints (No Auth Required)

- `GET /health` - Health check
- `GET /api/docs` - This documentation
- `GET /api/v1/openapi.json` - OpenAPI specification
- `GET /cas/:caId.pem` - CA certificate download
- `GET /crl/:caId.crl` - CRL download

## Rate Limiting
No rate limiting is currently enforced. This may change in future versions.

bearerAuth.description: JWT token obtained from OIDC provider (Keycloak, Auth0, Okta, etc.)

get.description: List Certificate Authorities with optional filtering, sorting, and pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

post.description: Create a new self-signed root Certificate Authority

get.description: Get detailed information about a specific Certificate Authority

delete.description: Delete a revoked or expired Certificate Authority. The CA must be revoked or expired and have no active certificates.

parameters.name: destroyKey
parameters.description: Also destroy the private key in KMS

post.description: Revoke a Certificate Authority. This will also revoke all certificates issued by this CA.

get.description: List all certificates issued by a specific Certificate Authority

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by subject DN, serial number, or SANs

get.description: List all Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs) for a specific CA

post.description: Generate a new Certificate Revocation List (CRL) for a specific CA

nextUpdateDays.description: Number of days until the CRL expires

get.description:
Download CA certificate in various formats.

**Certificate Formats (public certificate only):**
- `pem` - PEM text format (Base64-encoded with headers)
- `crt` - CRT text certificate (same as PEM)
- `der` - DER binary compact format
- `cer` - CER Windows-compatible format (same as DER)

**Truststore Formats (public certificate only, for trust validation):**
- `p12-truststore` - PKCS#12 truststore containing only the CA certificate
- `jks-truststore` - Java KeyStore truststore containing only the CA certificate

**Keystore Formats (certificate + private key, for CA signing operations):**
- `p12-keystore` - PKCS#12 keystore with CA certificate and private key
- `jks-keystore` - Java KeyStore with CA certificate and private key

**Security Note:** Keystore formats expose the CA's private key. Only use these for CA operations that require signing capability (e.g., offline CA scenarios).

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for P12 and JKS formats. Required for keystore formats, optional for truststore formats (defaults to "changeit" if not provided)

get.description: List certificates with optional filtering, sorting, and pagination

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by subject DN, serial number, or SANs

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

post.description: Issue a new certificate signed by a Certificate Authority

get.description: Get detailed information about a specific certificate

delete.description: Delete a revoked or expired certificate. Certificate must be revoked or expired for more than 90 days.

parameters.name: destroyKey
parameters.description: Also destroy the private key in KMS

post.description: Renew an existing certificate, optionally with new key or updated information

generateNewKey.description: Generate a new key pair for the renewed certificate

validityDays.description: Validity period in days (defaults to original validity)

updateInfo.description: Update subject or SANs with new values

revokeOriginal.description: Revoke the original certificate after renewal

effectiveDate.description: Unix timestamp for effective revocation date

generateCrl.description: Generate a new CRL after revocation

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for encrypted formats (P12, JKS, encrypted PKCS8)

defaultValidityDays.description: Default validity period in days if not specified per row

certificateIds.description: Array of certificate IDs to revoke

generateCrl.description: Generate a new CRL after revocation

certificateIds.description: Array of certificate IDs to renew

generateNewKey.description: Generate a new key pair for the renewed certificate

validityDays.description: Validity period in days (defaults to original validity)

revokeOriginal.description: Revoke the original certificate after renewal

delete.description: Bulk delete certificates (must be revoked or expired > 90 days)

certificateIds.description: Array of certificate IDs to delete

destroyKey.description: Also destroy the private key in KMS

certificateIds.description: Array of certificate IDs to download

encryptPrivateKey.description: Whether to encrypt private key with password
